revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Ishinuki Anakannon Yokoanabos]] {{Short description|Archaeological site in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name =Ishinuki Anakannon Yokoanabo | native_name =石貫穴観音横穴 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Kumamoto Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption =Ishinuki Anakannon Yokoanabo | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|32|58|09|N|130|33|47|E|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Tamana, Kumamoto|en|Tamana, Kumamoto|qid=Q861610|lt=Tamana, Kumamoto|1=Tamana, Kumamoto}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = {{nihongo|{{ill|corridor-type kofun|lt=cave tomb|kk=-type ''kofun''|ja|横穴墓|1=Yokoanabo|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corridor-type kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoanabo|qq=}}|横穴墓|Yokōbo}} | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = | abandoned = | epochs = | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes ={{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|32|58|09|N|130|33|47|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Ishinuki Anakannon Yokoanabos'''|石貫穴観音横穴|Ishinuki Anakannon yokoana}} is an {{ill|Archaeological site|en|Archaeological site|qid=Q839954|lt=Archaeological site|1=Archaeological site}} containing five [[Kofun period]] {{nihongo|{{ill|corridor-type kofun|lt=Yokoanabo|kk=-type ''kofun''|ja|横穴墓|1=Yokoanabo|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corridor-type kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoanabo|qq=}}|横穴墓|Yokōbo}} located in the Ishinuki neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Tamana, Kumamoto|en|Tamana, Kumamoto|qid=Q861610|lt=Tamana, Kumamoto|1=Tamana, Kumamoto}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. It was granted protection as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 1921.<ref name= "Bunka">{{cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/162548|title=石貫穴観音横穴 |work=Cultural Heritage Online|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|language=Japanese|accessdate=5 December 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Ishinuki Anakannon Yokoanabo is located about 5 kilometers north from the urban center of Tamana, just past Toratori Bridge over the Hanegi River. The cluster consists of five large corridor-type ''kofun'' on the west side of the same hill as the Ishinuki Yokoanabo Cluster, which has a separate National Historic Site designation. Tombs 1 to 3 are lined up in a row, and Tombs 4 and 5 are slightly further apart and lower. The first three tombs are [[decorated kofun]], and have triple-layered decorative borders at the entrance, with the outer two arched, and a faint red circle pattern. The central tomb has a carving of the {{ill|Avalokiteśvara|en|Avalokiteśvara|qid=Q193849|lt=Senjū Kannon|1=Avalokiteśvara}} (Thousand-armed Kannon) in the inner chamber, and an {{ill|Ekādaśamukha|en|Ekādaśamukha|qid=Q11404731|lt= Jūichimen Kannon|1=Ekādaśamukha}} to the right, so it is called the "Ana Kannon" (hole Kannon). This tomb consists of an antechamber and an inner chamber, the antechamber is almost square, 2.5 by 2.9 meters. The entrance to the inner chamber is covered with real tiles. The Thousand-Armed Kannon faith was introduced in the {{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|qid=Q189178|lt=Nara period|1=Nara period}}, and the cave may have to have been built in the first half of the 8th century, rather than having been a re-purposed Kofun period tomb.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kumamoto)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kumamoto)|qid=Q6573273|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kumamoto)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kumamoto)}}
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.city.tamana.lg.jp/q/aview/331/16290.html Tamana City home page] {{in lang|ja}}
